what_it_does: Documents the buyer-facing ConceptV Virtual Hub UI kit: its photo-first immersive layout, the overlay UI pieces, the component inventory, and how to run the demo.
observations: The viewer is photo-first, with architectural renders filling the viewport edge-to-edge.
UI sits on dark, slightly-translucent surfaces over the photo.
A bottom-centre MENU pill opens a Quick Menu with Info, Floorplan, Share, Gyro, and CLOSE options.
Right-side Info and Share panels are anchored at 360 px wide.
Top-left holds a brand bug plus a hub switcher (Entry / Apartment A / Apartment B / …).
A floating comment-capture popup is anchored to the point the user clicks on the photo.
The component table maps eight JSX files to their responsibilities.
index.html mounts the full hub experience for demo.
standouts: Panotour-style immersive property viewer with minimal dark overlay UI.
Click-anywhere capture-comment popup tied to a spatial point.
Hub switcher for navigating multiple units within the same viewer.
concerns: No accessibility notes or keyboard/screen-reader behavior.
No responsive or breakpoint behavior described.
No data contract or state flow for panels, comments, or hub switching.
No fallback for missing renders or slow-loading assets.
No i18n or copy localization guidance.
conventions: README organized as Layout / Components / Demo.
One component per JSX file with PascalCase names.
notes: This README is the design handoff for the Virtual Hub: it lays out where each UI element lives and which JSX file owns it, but leaves implementation details to the code.
questions: How is the hub switcher populated and what drives Entry / Apartment A / Apartment B?
What data feeds the InfoPanel title, features, description, and CTA?
How do capture comments persist or get submitted?
What does the Gyro menu item do and on which devices?
Which social platforms does SharePanel target?
Are the floorplan dots interactive or purely positional?
What are the transitions/animations for opening and swapping panels?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content: it is a UI kit README that describes layout, components, and demo usage.
apparent_themes: Spatial anchoring of controls and comments.
intention_signals: Provide a reusable, buyer-facing immersive property viewer that keeps the architectural render dominant while offering lightweight overlay controls and contextual feedback capture.
novelty: The capture-comment popup anchored to the exact clicked point inside a panotour-style viewer, plus a hub switcher for multiple apartments within one continuous experience.
contribution: It defines the Virtual Hub component inventory, spatial layout conventions, and user-interaction map (MENU trigger, right-side panels, full-bleed floorplan, click-to-comment) for a buyer-facing immersive property viewer.
summary_for_embedding: A README documenting the ConceptV Virtual Hub UI kit, a photo-first immersive property viewer with dark overlay controls, right-side panels, a hub switcher, and a click-to-comment popup.
intention_for_embedding: To hand off the layout, component responsibilities, and demo behavior for a buyer-facing virtual property tour hub.
